title: Research Radar
text:
This radar summarizes the thematic orientation of my research work. Scores do not represent article quality, journal rank, or scientific impact. They indicate the relative emphasis of each study across hydrological and methodological domains.










Interpretation


My current research profile is concentrated in surface hydrology, hydrometeorology, statistical hydrology, uncertainty analysis, and hydroinformatics. This reflects a methodological focus on open hydroclimatic datasets, rainfall-runoff modelling, bias correction, gap filling, regional constraints, and reproducible workflows for data-scarce basins.



The radar also shows the boundaries of this research line. Hydrogeology, water quality, and hydroecology are not central components of the studies represented here. This is not a weakness of the individual papers, but a deliberate delimitation of the research program.


Articles included



A1Comparativa de modelos tradicionales vs machine learning para el relleno de datos faltantes en series de caudales diarios en cuencas representativas del Perú


PublishedTecnología y Ciencias del Agua · DOI: 10.24850/j-tyca-18-02-02




A2Corrección de sesgo y transferencia estadística en series de precipitación máxima anual satelital en el río Piura


PublishedIngeniería del Agua · DOI: 10.4995/ia.24476




A3Bias-Corrected Precipitation Products in a Data-Scarce Andean Basin: Performance and Parameter Transferability


ManuscriptRBRH - Revista Brasileira de Recursos Hídricos · DOI: -




A4Uncertainty in Indirect IDF Estimation Using Open Precipitation Datasets


ManuscriptRevista Facultad de Ingeniería Universidad de Antioquia · DOI: -




A5Structural limitations of precipitation-only streamflow modelling in Andean basins under ENSO-driven and glacier-influenced regimes


ManuscriptHydrological Sciences Journal · DOI: -




A6Does daily performance guarantee annual reliability? Uncertainty propagation from gap-filled streamflow records into water balance estimation in data-scarce basins


ManuscriptWater Resources Management · DOI: -




A7GLUE-Limits of Acceptability Framework for Rainfall-Runoff Simulation in an Ungauged Arid Catchment: Regional Constraints and Precipitation-Product Evaluation


ManuscriptJournal of Hydrologic Engineering · DOI: -



Thematic pillars



Surface hydrology


Rainfall-runoff processes, streamflow estimation, hydrological signatures, and catchment response.




River hydraulics


Channel flow, routing, hydraulic interpretation, and flood-related applications.




Hydrogeology


Groundwater processes and aquifer-centered analysis.




Hydrometeorology


Precipitation, climate variability, satellite/reanalysis products, and extreme rainfall.




Water quality


Water chemistry, pollution, ecological indicators, and environmental water quality.




Water management


Planning, water balance, decision support, infrastructure, and resource management implications.




Statistical hydrology


Uncertainty, bias correction, frequency analysis, gap filling, and statistical methods.




Remote sensing / Hydroinformatics


Open datasets, reproducible coding, geospatial workflows, machine learning, and satellite products.



Scoring logic


The thematic scores range from 0 to 10 and describe the relative emphasis of each article across the eight pillars. A high score means that the pillar is central to the article. A low score means that the pillar is peripheral or absent.

title: Research Projects
text:
Assessment of uncertainty in indirect Intensity-Duration-Frequency (IDF) estimation and its propagation into peak discharge calculations in an ungauged hyperarid coastal basin of Peru.
Status: Ongoing



Assessment of the reliability of observed and satellite-based precipitation products for rainfall–runoff modelling and extreme event detection in the Cañete River Basin. Multiple calibration scenarios are evaluated using the GR4J model, with special emphasis on reproducing SENAMHI operational alert thresholds and high-flow events.
Status: Ongoing



Investigation of how uncertainty introduced by streamflow gap-filling methods propagates into annual water resources indicators. The study compares multiple reconstruction approaches across representative Peruvian basins and evaluates whether strong daily performance translates into reliable estimates of annual flow and water availability.
Status: Ongoing



Assessment of whether precipitation alone is sufficient to reproduce streamflow dynamics in Andean basins with contrasting hydrological controls. Using machine learning models and multiple precipitation datasets, the study investigates the ability of data-driven approaches to represent average conditions and extreme flows under ENSO-driven and glacier-influenced regimes.
Status: Ongoing

title: Research Projects
text:
Completed Projects

Satellite Precipitation Bias Correction
Bias correction and statistical transfer of satellite-derived annual maximum precipitation series for frequency analysis and extreme rainfall assessment in the Piura River Basin.
Status: Published (2026) DOI: 10.4995/ia.24476


Machine Learning Streamflow Reconstruction
Comparative evaluation of traditional statistical methods and machine learning algorithms for daily streamflow infilling in representative Peruvian river basins.
Status: Published (2026) DOI: 10.4995/ia.24476

title: About
text:
I hold a B.Sc. in Mechanical Fluids Engineering and work in hydrology, hydraulics and water resources analysis.
My research focuses on hydrological modelling, precipitation analysis, uncertainty assessment and environmental data science. I am particularly interested in understanding how data limitations, modelling choices and methodological assumptions influence hydrological predictions and decision-making in data-scarce environments.
My current research explores rainfall–runoff modelling, satellite and gridded precipitation products, extreme event analysis, and the application of statistical and machine learning methods in hydrology.
This website serves as a repository of publications, research projects, technical notes and reproducible workflows developed using R, GIS tools and open hydrological datasets.

title: Technical Notes
text: This section contains technical notes, tutorials, reproducible workflows and open-source tools related to hydrology, hydraulics and environmental data science.

title: Technical Notes
text:
Tools

Automated IDF Report Generator in R
An open-source workflow developed for automated frequency analysis of annual maximum precipitation series and generation of Intensity–Duration–Frequency (IDF) reports.
The toolkit includes an R script, an R Markdown template, example files and documentation required to generate station-based IDF reports automatically.

Features

Frequency analysis of annual maximum precipitation
Design rainfall estimation for multiple return periods
IDF curve generation
Automatic PDF report creation
Report output in Spanish



Download
Download IDF Toolkit (ZIP)

title: Home
text:
B.Sc. in Mechanical Fluids Engineering
Hydrology • Hydraulics • Water Resources
I am a Peruvian researcher and practitioner working in hydrology, hydraulics and environmental data science. My interests include hydrological modelling, precipitation analysis, uncertainty assessment, extreme events and data-driven approaches for water resources applications.
This website provides access to my publications, research projects, technical notes and reproducible workflows developed using open datasets, R and Quarto.




Understanding how data limitations, modelling assumptions and methodological choices influence hydrological predictions and water resources assessments.



Analysis of extreme rainfall, flood-generating processes and uncertainty in Intensity–Duration–Frequency (IDF) estimation for data-scarce basins.



Application of statistical and machine learning methods to streamflow reconstruction, rainfall–runoff modelling and environmental data analysis.





Published research on satellite precipitation bias correction and frequency analysis in the Piura River Basin.
Published comparative study of traditional and machine learning methods for daily streamflow infilling in Peruvian basins.
Ongoing research on IDF uncertainty propagation, rainfall–runoff modelling in the Cañete River Basin and hydrological data uncertainty.
